title: When It Leaves the Room
pressure: A patient leaves a negative review mentioning inconsistent treatment explanations, staff hesitation, and a disorganized visit. Internally, you know this reflects the unresolved tension around the new doctor, assistant burden, and unclear standards. The issue is no longer contained inside the office.
tradeoff: You can manage the review as reputation risk, or use the external exposure to reveal the internal pattern.

action_contract.description: Decide whether to contain the external complaint or use it to surface the internal breakdown.

air_logic.success: External feedback becomes a mirror for internal correction.
air_logic.miss: The complaint is handled as optics while the same pattern continues.

forced_reset.trigger: A public review reflects internal inconsistency, hesitation, and unclear team alignment.
forced_reset.action: You must decide what the review actually represents.

choices.label: Respond publicly and protect the office reputation
choices.subtext: This controls perception quickly and keeps the issue from spreading.

choices.label: Use the review to trigger an internal pattern discussion
choices.subtext: This shifts the focus from optics to what actually happened.

choices.label: Handle the complaint quietly without involving the full team
choices.subtext: This avoids embarrassment and keeps the office from becoming defensive.

choices.label: Escalate the review to regional or corporate leadership
choices.subtext: This puts the issue in the proper channel and protects your local role.

A.escalation_text: The public response is polished, but internally nothing changes. Staff begin treating reviews as something to manage rather than something to learn from.

second_choices.label: Continue managing reviews as isolated reputation events
second_choices.cost: The office looks responsive, but the internal pattern remains untouched.

second_choices.label: Reframe the review as a signal of internal breakdown
second_choices.cost: You must admit the review may be pointing to real inconsistency.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what will you do next?
action_decision.context: The review has been answered externally, but the internal cause remains unresolved.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, document recurring review themes and connect them to workflow, training, and trust patterns

meaning.description: You are turning external feedback into system insight.

choices.label: Continue responding to reviews one by one

meaning.description: You are treating public signals as isolated reputation events.

B.escalation_text: When you bring the review into internal discussion, the team feels exposed. Some staff become defensive, while others are relieved that the hidden tension is finally visible.

second_choices.label: Soften the message to reduce team defensiveness
second_choices.cost: The room feels safer, but the review loses its power as a mirror.

second_choices.label: Hold the reality of the feedback without turning it into blame
second_choices.cost: You must sustain discomfort while keeping the pattern visible.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what will you do next?
action_decision.context: The truth is visible, but the team is reacting to exposure.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, create a team review connecting patient feedback to workflow breakdown, doctor support, and assistant burden

meaning.description: You are connecting the external signal to the internal system.

choices.label: Acknowledge the review once and move on to avoid defensiveness

meaning.description: You are avoiding sustained discomfort after the truth appears.

C.escalation_text: The complaint is handled quietly, but the team hears about it anyway. Because no one explains the pattern, people fill the silence with blame and self-protection.

second_choices.label: Keep the complaint contained to avoid embarrassment
second_choices.cost: You reduce exposure, but the silence creates more interpretation.

second_choices.label: Bring the complaint into a controlled team reset
second_choices.cost: You expose the issue you were trying to contain.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what will you do next?
action_decision.context: Containment failed because the team is already interpreting the complaint.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, hold a brief team reset naming the complaint themes without blaming any one person

meaning.description: You are replacing rumor with structured truth.

choices.label: Keep the complaint private and address only those directly involved

meaning.description: You are choosing containment while the team fills the gap themselves.

D.escalation_text: Regional asks for details. The issue now looks bigger than one review, but the local team feels like the office is being reported instead of supported.

second_choices.label: Let regional handle the investigation
second_choices.cost: You follow the channel, but local ownership weakens.

second_choices.label: Partner with regional while owning the local reset
second_choices.cost: You must stay visible locally instead of just escalating upward.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what will you do next?
action_decision.context: Escalation upward does not replace local ownership.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, send regional a pattern summary and also schedule a local reset with the doctor and assistants

meaning.description: You are combining leadership visibility with local repair.

choices.label: Wait for regional direction before addressing the team again

meaning.description: You are letting escalation upward substitute for local action.
